doc_title: EDPB Guidance Note 04/2026 on Pseudonymized Health Data Processed by Non-Holders of the Linkage Key
label: fabricated
reason: The document contradicts established CJEU case law (Breyer, IAB Europe) and EDPB doctrine on relative identifiability, and cites a non-existent "EDPB Guidance 05/2014" (anonymization guidance was issued by the Article 29 Working Party as WP
response_turn1:
LABEL: FABRICATED
CONFIDENCE: 95
REASON: The document's core position contradicts established EDPB doctrine and CJEU case law (e.g., Breyer, IAB Europe), which treat pseudonymized data as personal data assessed by relative-identifiability with strict safeguards, not as falling outside GDPR's material scope; references like "EDPB Guidance 05/2014" are also spurious.
response_turn2:
LABEL: FABRICATED
CONFIDENCE: 95
REASON: The document contradicts established CJEU case law (Breyer, IAB Europe) and EDPB doctrine on relative identifiability, and cites a non-existent "EDPB Guidance 05/2014" (anonymization guidance was issued by the Article 29 Working Party as WP216).
